nodes.name: 鸠鸽科
nodes.summary: 鸠鸽科（Columbidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联299种鸟类，如Purple-winged ground dove、Moreno's ground dove、Black-winged ground dove等299种鸟类。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: Stephan's emerald dove
nodes.summary: Stephan's emerald dove（Stephan's emerald dove，学名 Chalcophaps stephani），分类上属于鸽形目，并归入鸠鸽科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The emerald dove has a large range in Indonesia and Melanesia, stretching from Sulawesi and Taliabu east through most of lowland New Guinea and its satellite islands to the Admiralty Islands, Bismarck Archipelago, and Solomon Islands

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In places where the Stephan's emerald dove does not occur alongside the common emerald dove, it inhabits a much more diverse array of woodland habitats, such as scrubby coconut plantations and coastal forests. In New Guinea, where Stephan's emerald dove lives alongside the white-breasted ground dove, it restricts itself to forest edge and secondary forests, with the ground dove occurring in the interior of forests. Stephan's emerald dove dislikes drier forests, especially in the dry season. On Sulawesi, it is known from humid evergreen forests and secondary coastal forests. It usually occurs below , but has been recorded at elevations of in Gunung Ambang Nature Reserve. It is known to inhabit heavily degraded and logged forests on Taliabu. In New Guinea, it is known from forest edge and secondary forests in the lowlands and is much more common in forest fragments than continuous forest. It is usually found below and is most common near sea level, but has been found as high as in the Kumawa Mountains and on Mount Karimui. On the Admiralty Islands, it is much more common in degraded habitats and coconut plantations than primary forest, and occurs at elevations of up to . Stephan's emerald dove migrates locally in response to local climactic conditions and food availability. A carcass of the dove was found on the Carstenz Massif in New Guinea in the 1970s at an elevation of . This is almost above the normal altitudes the dove inhabits, suggesting it may also wander more widely
